meta.mandate: Deliver fully interactive verify/shell terminals as CaptureSession-based split-view tabs with keyboard+mouse forwarding. All terminals (Claude, Verify, Shell) must be FULLY INTERACTIVE including mouse. Use termmux CaptureSession (higher-level), not raw PTY.
meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: PIPE ALL BUILD OUTPUT: All make/go commands MUST be piped through `| tail -n N` or `| fold -w 200 | tail -n N` to prevent context explosion. Use `make make-all-with-log` for full suite which logs to build.log.
TIMEOUT: Full test suite requires `-timeout=20m`. Use `make test-only` which sets this. Individual package tests: `go test -timeout=300s ./internal/command/...`

CHUNK LOADING ORDER: JS chunks in pr_split.go are loaded sequentially via prSplitChunks array. When splitting files, new chunks MUST be inserted in correct order and all //go:embed directives + array entries updated.
RULE OF TWO: Every task MUST pass strict-review-gate (2 contiguous subagent reviews on identical diff) BEFORE commit. Each task is committed individually.
NO DEAD CODE: deadcode linter runs in CI. Any new exports/functions MUST be wired. Never add to .deadcodeignore. Wire it or delete it.
CROSS-PLATFORM: Code must work on Linux, macOS, and Windows. PTY-dependent features (shell integration) MUST have //go:build unix guard and graceful degradation on Windows.

⚠️ PER-TASK COMMIT IS MANDATORY: After completing EACH task: (1) Reread strict-review-gate Skill in full, (2) Rule of Two until 2 contiguous green reviews, (3) Read git-commit Skill, (4) Commit THAT SINGLE TASK immediately, (5) Reread DIRECTIVE.txt. NEVER batch tasks. NEVER skip reviews. NEVER defer commits.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are grouped into 6 blocks (A-F). Blocks A-B fix production bugs and should be done first. Block D is parallel investigation. Block E splits files before Block C adds new code. Block F restructures tests. Block C (largest) implements shell integration on the now-refactored codebase.
mandatoryDirectives.commitPolicy: Each task is committed individually after passing the Rule of Two. Use Kubernetes-style commit messages via the git-commit Skill.
mandatoryDirectives.testPolicy: Every code change MUST include tests in its acceptance criteria. Run `make make-all-with-log` after every commit to verify no regressions.
mandatoryDirectives.PER_TASK_COMMIT_PROTOCOL: ⚠️ MANDATORY — ZERO EXCEPTIONS — FOR EVERY SINGLE TASK: (1) REREAD the strict-review-gate Skill IN FULL — do not skim, do not skip, read every word. (2) PERFORM the Rule of Two PER THE SKILL — spawn subagent reviews, iterate until TWO CONTIGUOUS ISSUE-FREE reviews on the EXACT SAME DIFF. If either review fails, fix, reset counter to 0, restart. (3) READ the git-commit Skill IN FULL before writing any commit message. (4) COMMIT THAT ONE TASK — exactly one task per commit, immediately after passing the Rule of Two. Do not batch tasks. Do not defer commits. ASAP. (5) REREAD DIRECTIVE.txt after every commit — full re-read, not from memory. This 5-step process is NON-NEGOTIABLE. Skipping any step is grounds for termination. Batching multiple tasks into one commit is FORBIDDEN. Committing without two green reviews is FORBIDDEN. Working on the next task before committing the current one is FORBIDDEN.

statusSection.goalState: All verify/shell terminal features delivered AND polished to enterprise grade. Zero silenced errors. No god functions. No magic numbers. Fuzz-tested parsing. Poll-retry E2E tests. Fully parallelized unit tests. Documentation fully synced. All checks pass on all platforms.

replanLog.note: T303+T304+T305 combined into single commit — identical fix pattern (+ → joinHorizontal) across 3 screens

FULL REPLAN of T321-T335. Previous approach used raw PTY spawning (spawnPTY + mux.attach + mux.switchTo = blocking passthrough). Correct approach: CaptureSession-based tabs in split-view with screen() for display, write() for input, mouseToTermBytes() for mouse. Reverted all WIP raw-PTY code. Expanded scope to T321-T349 covering verify tab, shell tab, mouse forwarding, resize propagation, E2E tests, golden files, docs, and benchmarks.

T349 finalization: Fixed test timeout 12m→20m, help overlay test assertion. Expanded scope T360-T371 covering: scratch cleanup, catch block logging, magic number extraction, E2E flakiness (poll-retry), fuzz tests, automatedSplit refactoring, comment normalization, wizard integration test fixes, duplicate test file cleanup.

sequentialTasks.task: Document failure modes in scratch/failure.md from real CLI execution
sequentialTasks.description: Run osm pr-split with real CLI flags (-claude-command, -claude-arg) in a test context and document what happens. Create scratch/failure.md with screenshots/output of any failures, UX issues, or missing functionality.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: scratch/failure.md exists with documented observations. All identified issues tracked in blueprint.

sequentialTasks.task: Add E2E test validating full CLI flag pipeline (-claude-command, -claude-arg, -claude-model)
sequentialTasks.description: Create automated test that validates the complete flow: flag parsing → JS config → ClaudeCodeExecutor construction → command resolution → spawn with correct args. Must verify args are concatenated correctly with MCP config path.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: E2E test exists in pr_split test files. Test spawns with mock command and verifies arg order. Test passes in CI.

sequentialTasks.description: Remove git-stage-t376, git-diff-staged, and any other temporary targets added during this session.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: config.mk contains no task-specific temporary targets. make help shows clean target list.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit verify input forwarding: ensure arrow/modifier keys map correctly through keyToTermBytes
sequentialTasks.description: The verify tab forwards keys via keyToTermBytes(k). Audit that all terminal-relevant keys (arrows, function keys, ctrl+sequences, escape) are correctly mapped. Compare against Claude tab's input path.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Audit document in scratch/. All critical keys verified. Any missing mappings added with tests.

sequentialTasks.task: Add resize propagation to verify CaptureSession on WindowSize events
sequentialTasks.description: When terminal resizes, verify CaptureSession needs session.resize(rows, cols) called. Check if this is already wired in the WindowSize handler.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WindowSize handler calls session.resize() for activeVerifySession. Test verifies resize propagation.

sequentialTasks.task: Extract automatedSplit from god function (deferred — risky)
sequentialTasks.description: Large extraction from automatedSplit function. Deferred due to high risk of regression.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: automatedSplit reduced to orchestration calls. Sub-functions tested independently.

continuousVerification.task: Run project-defined build and test commands after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: After each code change: `make make-all-with-log 2>&1 | tail -n 30`. After each PR Split change: `go test -timeout=300s -v ./internal/command/... -run '<relevant_pattern>' 2>&1 | tail -n 40`. After each commit: full `make make-all-with-log`.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: All new code must have unit tests. All visual changes must have render tests. Shell integration must have E2E tests. Aim for 100% branch coverage on new functions.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: PIPE ALL BUILD OUTPUT through tail/fold to prevent context explosion. Use make make-all-with-log.
RULE OF TWO: Every task requires 2 contiguous subagent reviews on identical diff before commit. No exceptions.
TIMEOUT: Use -timeout=20m for full suite. Individual packages: -timeout=300s. Binary E2E: -timeout=120s per test.
CHUNK LOADING ORDER: After splitting JS files, verify prSplitChunks in pr_split.go loads in correct dependency order.

⚠️⚠️⚠️ PER-TASK COMMIT PROTOCOL — EXECUTE FOR EVERY TASK WITHOUT EXCEPTION: Step 1: REREAD strict-review-gate Skill (FULL TEXT, not from memory). Step 2: PERFORM Rule of Two (2 contiguous green subagent reviews on identical diff — iterate until achieved). Step 3: READ git-commit Skill (FULL TEXT). Step 4: COMMIT that ONE task IMMEDIATELY (never batch). Step 5: REREAD DIRECTIVE.txt (FULL TEXT). VIOLATION = TERMINATION. This is Hana-san's direct order.
